Gemini Enters the Voice Synthesis Game

In March 2026, Google launched the Gemini 2.5 Pro TTS preview — a new text-to-speech model optimized for quality. It represents a significant leap beyond traditional Google Cloud TTS in expressiveness and naturalness.

While previous TTS models were tools that converted text to audio, Gemini 2.5 Pro TTS is an AI that understands the intent of text and speaks naturally.

Key Features

Enhanced Expressivity

The model understands emotion and nuance, automatically applying context-appropriate intonation. Whether it's reading news, narrating a story, or delivering a business presentation, the tone adjusts accordingly.

Precision Pacing

It understands sentence structure and inserts pauses at the right places — not just at commas and periods, but at semantic boundaries, creating a natural speaking rhythm.

Seamless Dialogue

The ability to naturally differentiate speakers in conversational text has been dramatically improved. In multi-speaker dialogues, each character gets distinct vocal qualities and tones.

Using the API

Basic TTS Request

Style Control

Control the reading tone through prompts:

Use Cases

Auto-Generated Podcasts

Convert blog posts and reports into podcast-style audio content. Similar to NotebookLM's Audio Overview, but directly accessible via API.

Multilingual Apps

When building apps with voice output in Japanese, English, Chinese, and more, Gemini TTS delivers native-quality speech for each language.

Accessibility

Great potential for screen readers and educational tools. The audio quality is significantly more listenable compared to traditional TTS.

Compared to Cloud TTS

| Feature | Gemini 2.5 Pro TTS | Cloud TTS | |---------|-------------------|-----------| | Emotion | Auto-adjusts from context | Manual SSML tags | | Dialogue | Natural speaker separation | Single speaker | | Customization | Prompt-based | SSML-based | | Pricing | API pay-as-you-go | Per-character |

Things to Note

  • Currently in preview — exercise caution for production use
  • Real-time audio streaming is planned for a future update
  • Audio quality may vary for some languages

Takeaway

Gemini 2.5 Pro TTS sets a new standard for AI voice synthesis. Its prompt-based style control and context-aware natural speech are its standout features. It's easy to try through the API — give it a spin on your next project.

en.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro Complete Guide: Mastering Complex Tasks with the Three-Tier Thinking System
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: A complete guide to Gemini 3.1 Pro, released February 2026. Explore the three-tier thinking system, 77.1% ARC-AGI-2 reasoning score, 1M token context, and hands-on API examples.
en.content:
Introduction

On February 19, 2026, Google released Gemini 3.1 Pro as a public preview — the latest and most capable model in the Gemini family. This release brings two headline improvements over Gemini 3 Pro: a dramatic leap in reasoning performance and a new three-tier thinking system that gives developers fine-grained control over computation depth.

In this guide, we'll walk through what makes Gemini 3.1 Pro special and how to start using it today.

What Is Gemini 3.1 Pro?

Gemini 3.1 Pro is Google DeepMind's flagship model designed for complex problem-solving, agentic workflows, and advanced coding tasks. It's available across Gemini API (AI Studio), Vertex AI, Gemini Enterprise, Gemini CLI, and Android Studio.

Model Specs at a Glance

| Spec | Details | |------|---------| | Model ID | gemini-3.1-pro-preview | | Released | February 19, 2026 | | Context Window | Up to 1,000,000 tokens | | Supported Modalities | Text, images, audio, video, PDFs, code repositories | | Reasoning (ARC-AGI-2) | 77.1% — more than double Gemini 3 Pro |

The Headline Feature: Three-Tier Thinking System

The most important addition in Gemini 3.1 Pro is the three-tier thinking budget system. Previous Gemini models offered only a binary choice between fast (low compute) and deep (high compute) modes. Gemini 3.1 Pro introduces a Medium tier, allowing developers to strike a mathematically balanced trade-off between response latency and reasoning depth.

Choosing the Right Thinking Mode

| Mode | Characteristics | Best For | |------|----------------|----------| | Low | Fast, low cost | Q&A, text generation, simple tasks | | Medium | Balanced | Summarization, code review, standard reasoning | | High | Deep, precise | Math proofs, complex agent workflows |

Getting Started: Calling the API

Basic Python Example

Specifying the Thinking Budget

REST API Call

Reasoning Breakthrough: 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2

Gemini 3.1 Pro achieved 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2, a rigorous benchmark designed to test genuine general reasoning — patterns a model has never seen before. This is more than double the score of Gemini 3 Pro and represents a top-tier result at the time of release.

ARC-AGI-2 is considered especially hard because it demands novel pattern recognition and logic that can't be memorized — making this score a meaningful signal that AI is approaching more flexible, human-like reasoning.

Multimodal Capabilities in Practice

Gemini 3.1 Pro handles text, images, audio, video, PDFs, and full code repositories through its unified 1M-token context window.

Analyzing an Image

Parsing a PDF Document

Where to Access Gemini 3.1 Pro

For Developers

  • Google AI Studio — Try it free in preview
  • Gemini API (gemini-3.1-pro-preview)
  • Vertex AI (enterprise-grade)
  • Gemini CLI

For End Users

  • Gemini app (Google AI Pro / Ultra plans)
  • NotebookLM (Pro / Ultra plans only)
  • GitHub Copilot (public preview)

FAQ

Q: How is Gemini 3.1 Pro different from Gemini 2.5 Pro? A: 3.1 Pro is a newer generation entirely — part of the Gemini 3 series that succeeded the 2.x line. Key differences include the three-tier thinking system and significantly improved reasoning benchmarks.

Q: Is it free to use? A: You can access it for free via Google AI Studio with an API key during the preview period, subject to rate limits.

Q: When will gemini-3.1-pro-preview become generally available? A: Google is validating the preview before a full GA release. Check the official changelog for the latest updates.

Q: Does Gemini 3.1 Pro support languages other than English? A: Yes — it supports multiple languages including Japanese, with high-quality, natural-sounding output in each.

Conclusion

Gemini 3.1 Pro sets a new bar with its three-tier thinking system and unprecedented ARC-AGI-2 reasoning scores. Whether you're building agentic pipelines, multimodal applications, or tackling complex analytical tasks, this model gives you the flexibility and power to handle it all.

Get started by grabbing an API key from Google AI Studio and experimenting with gemini-3.1-pro-preview. Try switching between thinking modes to find the right balance of speed and depth for your specific use case.

References: Gemini 3.1 Pro Official Blog / Gemini API Docs

en.title: Getting Started with Gemini 3 Computer Use — AI That Operates Your Browser
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: Learn how to use the Computer Use tool in Gemini 3 Flash and Pro. Build AI agents that autonomously navigate browsers and UIs — with code examples and real-world scenarios.
en.content:
Getting Started with Gemini 3 Computer Use — AI That Operates Your Browser

Introduction

In March 2026, Google introduced Computer Use tool support for Gemini 3 Flash Preview and Gemini 3 Pro Preview. This breakthrough enables developers to build computer-operating agents — AI systems that can look at a screenshot and autonomously navigate a browser or desktop UI by clicking, typing, and scrolling just like a human would.

This is fundamentally different from traditional browser automation tools like Selenium or Playwright scripting. Rather than following a rigid set of pre-programmed instructions, Gemini's Computer Use understands the content of the screen and decides on the most appropriate action in real time, adapting to unexpected changes and dynamic interfaces.

In this guide, we'll cover everything you need to know: what Computer Use is, which models support it, how to implement it, and real-world scenarios where it shines.

What Is the Computer Use Tool?

The Computer Use tool allows Gemini to receive a screenshot of a screen, reason about what it sees, and return a structured action — such as a mouse click at specific coordinates, keyboard input, or a scroll gesture. Your application then executes the action and feeds the next screenshot back to Gemini, creating an autonomous feedback loop.

Supported Action Types

| Action | Description | |--------|-------------| | click | Click at specified (x, y) coordinates | | type | Type a string of text | | key | Send a keyboard shortcut or key press | | scroll | Scroll in a given direction at given coordinates | | screenshot | Request a new screenshot (for loop continuation) | | move | Move the mouse cursor without clicking |

Supported Models

Computer Use is available on the following Gemini 3 models as of March 2026:

| Model | Characteristics | |-------|----------------| | gemini-3-flash-preview | Fast and cost-efficient. Ideal for agentic workflows | | gemini-3-pro-preview | Highest accuracy. Best for complex UI interactions |

Note: In the Gemini 2.5 series, Computer Use required a dedicated gemini-2.5-pro-preview-computer-use model. With Gemini 3, the capability is built directly into both Flash and Pro, simplifying your setup considerably.

Prerequisites

  • Basic Python knowledge
  • A Gemini API key (available via Google AI Studio)
  • A browser automation library: Playwright (local) or Browserbase (cloud)

Step 1: Installation

Step 2: The Core Computer Use Loop

The heart of Computer Use is a simple loop: capture screenshot → send to Gemini → receive action → execute → repeat.

Step 3: Building a Full Browser Agent with Playwright

Real-World Use Cases

Use Case 1: Automated Form Filling

Stop copy-pasting data from spreadsheets into web forms. Let Gemini do it for you:

Use Case 2: Web Research Agent

Gemini can browse multiple pages, extract information, and synthesize findings:

Use Case 3: Automated UI Testing

Replace fragile selector-based tests with visual, human-like verification:

Best Practices

Write Specific Task Descriptions

Vague instructions lead to unpredictable behavior. Instead of "click the button," say "click the blue Submit button in the top-right corner of the form." The more specific you are, the more reliably Gemini will understand the goal.

Always Set a Maximum Step Limit

Never run the agent loop without a max_steps guard. Edge cases like captchas, unexpected pop-ups, or ambiguous UI states can otherwise lead to indefinite loops.

Add Confirmation Steps for Critical Actions

For irreversible actions — form submissions, purchases, deletions — insert a confirmation prompt before execution. This prevents unintended consequences when the AI misinterprets context.

Implement Robust Error Handling

Unexpected UI changes happen. Wrap action execution in try-except blocks and optionally feed the error state screenshot back to Gemini to let it self-recover:

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How is this different from Gemini 2.5's Computer Use? A: Gemini 2.5 required a separate dedicated model (gemini-2.5-pro-preview-computer-use). With Gemini 3, Computer Use is natively integrated into both Flash and Pro, so you don't need a separate model endpoint.

Q: Can I run this locally? A: Absolutely. With Playwright installed locally, the agent controls a real browser on your machine. For cloud-based execution, Browserbase is a popular option.

Q: Is there an additional charge for Computer Use? A: No extra fee. Standard Gemini API token pricing applies. Screenshots are billed as multimodal (image) inputs.

Q: What about security? Can the agent access sensitive data? A: The agent only sees what's on screen. Use sandboxed browser environments for sensitive workflows, and never run an untrusted task prompt with access to authenticated sessions containing sensitive information.

Q: Does it support video input instead of screenshots? A: Currently, the primary input is still screenshots. For fluid interaction, you can capture screenshots at high frequency. Native video support may come in future releases.

Conclusion

Gemini 3's Computer Use tool represents a major leap forward in AI agent capabilities. Whether you want to automate repetitive form-filling, power visual UI testing, or build a fully autonomous research assistant, the foundation is now accessible to any developer with a Gemini API key.

Start with gemini-3-flash-preview for rapid prototyping — its speed and cost make it the ideal model for iteration. When you need higher accuracy for complex, multi-step interfaces, upgrade to gemini-3-pro-preview.

The era of AI that can truly "use a computer" is here. What will you build?

Resources:

  • Official sample repo: google-gemini/computer-use-preview
  • Gemini 3 Flash Preview docs: Google AI for Developers
  • Google AI Studio (get your API key): aistudio.google.com

en.title: Complete Guide to Gemini 2.5 Flash — The Hybrid Model That Balances Speed and Reasoning
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: A thorough guide to Gemini 2.5 Flash's features, pricing, and API usage. Learn how to control reasoning depth with thinking_budget for optimal cost and performance.
en.content:
Introduction

Gemini 2.5 Flash is Google's next-generation AI model that achieves an exceptional balance of speed, cost-efficiency, and reasoning capability. Since its release in 2025, it has earned strong praise from developers — particularly for its hybrid design that lets you toggle "thinking" on or off as needed.

In this guide, we'll walk through Gemini 2.5 Flash's key features, how to use the API, and practical strategies for managing cost.

What Is Gemini 2.5 Flash?

Gemini 2.5 Flash is developed by Google DeepMind and sits in the fastest, most cost-efficient tier of the Gemini 2.5 series. It's not simply a "lite" version of the Pro model — it delivers reasoning capability that approaches Gemini 2.5 Pro while dramatically reducing latency and cost.

Key Features

  • Hybrid reasoning model: The world's first production-ready model allowing you to toggle thinking via the thinking_budget parameter
  • 1M token context window: Process long documents, codebases, and conversation history in full
  • Multimodal input: Handles text, images, video, audio, and PDFs seamlessly
  • Low cost: Significantly cheaper than Gemini 2.5 Pro

Specifications

| Item | Value | |------|-------| | Context window | 1,048,576 tokens | | Max output tokens | 65,536 tokens | | Input pricing | $0.15 / 1M tokens (no thinking) | | Output pricing | $0.60 / 1M tokens (no thinking) | | Thinking token pricing | $0.35 / 1M tokens | | Supported modalities | Text / Image / Video / Audio / PDF |

Prerequisites

Before diving in, it helps to have the following:

  • Basic familiarity with Python or JavaScript
  • Experience using REST APIs or SDKs
  • An API key (available for free from Google AI Studio)

Setup

Install the Python SDK

Set Your Environment Variable

Basic Usage

Text Generation (Without Thinking)

The simplest use case is specifying the gemini-2.5-flash model and generating text.

Streaming Responses

For long outputs, use streaming to receive content in real time.

Using the Thinking Feature

The standout capability of Gemini 2.5 Flash is the thinking_budget parameter. By setting a maximum number of tokens for internal reasoning, you can precisely balance quality against cost and latency.

thinking_budget Reference

| Value | Behavior | |-------|----------| | 0 | No thinking (fastest, cheapest) | | 1–1024 | Light reasoning | | 1025–8192 | Moderate reasoning | | 8193–24576 | Deep reasoning (highest quality) |

Setting a thinking_budget

Inspecting the Thinking Process

Setting include_thoughts=True lets you retrieve the model's internal reasoning — useful for debugging or educational purposes.

Multimodal Input

Gemini 2.5 Flash can process far more than text.

Image Analysis

PDF Analysis

Cost Optimization Best Practices

Recommended thinking_budget by Use Case

| Use Case | Recommended Budget | Reason | |----------|-------------------|--------| | Chat / Q&A | 0–1024 | Speed matters most | | Code generation | 2048–8192 | Logical consistency required | | Math / Puzzles | 8192–24576 | Deep reasoning is beneficial | | Document summarization | 0–512 | Simple extraction task | | Agents / Planning | 4096–16384 | Multi-step planning needed |

Context Caching

If you're reusing the same system prompt repeatedly, leverage context caching — cached input tokens are billed at 25% of the standard rate, which can significantly reduce costs in high-volume applications.

FAQ

Q: When should I choose Gemini 2.5 Flash over Gemini 2.5 Pro?

A: Choose Flash when cost and speed are priorities. For tasks requiring the absolute highest accuracy — such as advanced coding or complex multi-step reasoning — Pro may be more appropriate. That said, raising Flash's thinking_budget can bring it very close to Pro quality for many tasks.

Q: What's the difference between thinking_budget=0 and just not using thinking?

A: Setting thinking_budget=0 explicitly disables the thinking process, resulting in the fastest and cheapest mode of operation. This is ideal for simple Q&A, text transformation, or any task that doesn't require deep reasoning.

Q: Can I really use the full 1M token context window?

A: Yes. However, latency and cost increase with token count. It's most valuable for processing long documents or large codebases, but you should avoid unnecessarily long contexts to keep things efficient.

Q: Can I call it via REST without an SDK?

A: Absolutely. You can use cURL or any HTTP client. The endpoint is https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.5-flash:generateContent.

Summary

Gemini 2.5 Flash stands among the best available models in terms of balancing speed, cost, and reasoning power. The thinking_budget feature gives you fine-grained control to match model behavior to your task complexity, enabling substantial cost savings.

With broad support for chat, agents, document analysis, and multimodal workflows, a solid strategy is to start with Flash and upgrade to Pro only when the task truly demands it — keeping development costs low from the start.

References

  • Gemini 2.5 Flash — Google AI for Developers
  • Gemini API — Google AI Studio
  • Start building with Gemini 2.5 Flash — Google Developers Blog

en.title: Gemini Deep Research Agent Guide — Building Autonomous Research with the Interactions API
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: Learn how to use Gemini's new Deep Research Agent via the Interactions API. Build autonomous multi-step research workflows with document analysis, web grounding, and cited report generation.
en.content:
What Is the Gemini Deep Research Agent?

In March 2026, Google launched the Deep Research Agent as a preview feature in the Gemini API. It is an AI agent capable of autonomously planning, executing, and synthesizing multi-step research tasks.

Unlike traditional RAG or search grounding, the Deep Research Agent can:

  • Automatically create multi-step research plans
  • Gather information across both the web and provided documents
  • Generate detailed reports with full citations
  • Run asynchronously in the background for long-duration tasks

This gives you an end-to-end autonomous research capability in a single API call.

Requirements

The Deep Research Agent is only accessible via the Interactions API (Beta).

  • Underlying model: gemini-3.1-pro
  • API: Interactions API (Beta)
  • Execution: Asynchronous background execution

Setup

First, update the google-genai package to the latest version:

Basic Usage

1. Simple Research Request

2. Research with Document Uploads

You can upload local PDF or CSV files and combine them with live web data.

Production-Grade Async Pattern

For long-running research tasks, an async polling pattern is recommended over blocking calls.

Customizing Output

JSON Schema Output

Specify a response_schema to receive structured results:

Control Output Structure via Prompt

You can also define the output format directly in your prompt without a schema:

Pricing

Deep Research Agent costs depend on the depth of research the agent autonomously performs.

| Component | Billing Basis | |-----------|--------------| | Input tokens | Gemini 3.1 Pro rate ($1.25/M tokens) | | Output tokens | Gemini 3.1 Pro rate ($10.00/M tokens) | | Web searches | Per search call | | File searches | Based on file size and processing |

Since the agent runs multiple searches across multiple steps, clearly scoping your research request in the prompt is the most effective way to control costs.

Deep Research Agent vs. generate_content

| Feature | generate_content | Deep Research Agent | |---------|----------------|---------------------| | Execution | Synchronous | Async background | | Autonomous planning | No | Yes (multi-step) | | Web search | Manual configuration | Auto-determined | | Document analysis | Manual | Automatic cross-referencing | | Execution time | Seconds | Minutes to tens of minutes | | Cost | Low–Medium | Medium–High (complexity-dependent) |

Practical Use Cases

Automated Competitive Intelligence Schedule regular competitor analysis and deliver reports to Slack or email automatically.

Technology Trend Monitoring Survey the latest developments in a specific domain daily and aggregate into a dashboard.

Regulatory Change Tracking Monitor changes in industry regulations automatically and alert compliance teams.

Investment Research Assistance Automate the initial research phase of company analysis (with humans making final decisions).

Important Caveats

  • The Deep Research Agent is currently in preview — use with caution in production
  • Always fact-check research results; accuracy is not guaranteed
  • Background execution requires polling or webhooks to retrieve results

Conclusion

The Gemini Deep Research Agent is a powerful feature that automates complex, multi-step research workflows through a single API. Combined with the Interactions API's background execution, it dramatically simplifies automated report generation and information aggregation. Take advantage of the preview period to explore use cases and prepare for production adoption.

en.title: Gemini 2.5 Pro vs 2.5 Flash — Model Selection Guide by Use Case
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: Compare Gemini 2.5 Pro and Flash models. Explore performance, speed, cost, context windows, and practical use cases.
en.content:
Choosing Between Gemini 2.5 Pro and Flash

Google's Gemini model family offers multiple variants designed for different use cases. Understanding the performance and cost tradeoffs between Gemini 2.5 Pro and the fast Gemini 2.5 Flash is essential for building efficient AI-powered applications.

Core Model Characteristics

Gemini 2.5 Pro Strengths

Gemini 2.5 Pro is engineered for advanced reasoning and complex task processing with these defining features:

  • Reasoning Capability: Excels at complex logic problems, mathematical reasoning, and multi-step problem solving
  • Context Window: 200,000 tokens supports analysis of lengthy documents, codebases, and research papers
  • Code Generation: Superior ability to generate, debug, and optimize software code with architectural understanding
  • Multimodal Analysis: Handles complex tasks involving images, videos, and audio with sophisticated interpretation

Response latency averages 3-5 seconds, making it ideal when accuracy is the priority.

Gemini 2.5 Flash Strengths

Gemini 2.5 Flash prioritizes speed and efficiency for real-time applications:

  • Response Speed: Average latency of 0.5-1 second—5 to 10 times faster than Pro
  • Cost Efficiency: API pricing is approximately 60-70% lower per token compared to Pro
  • Context Window: 100,000 tokens covers most practical use cases effectively
  • Optimized Inference: Fine-tuned for scenarios requiring immediate responses

Detailed Performance Comparison

| Metric | Gemini 2.5 Pro | Gemini 2.5 Flash | |--------|---|---| | Context Window | 200,000 tokens | 100,000 tokens | | Avg Response Time | 3-5 seconds | 0.5-1 second | | Reasoning Quality | Exceptional | High | | Input Cost (per M tokens) | $7.50 | $2.50 | | Output Cost (per M tokens) | $30.00 | $10.00 | | Complex Reasoning | Excellent | Strong | | Standard Text Tasks | Overkill | Sufficient |

When to Choose Gemini 2.5 Pro

Complex Mathematical Problem Solving

For research-grade mathematics involving intricate notation and multi-step derivations:

Software Architecture Design Consultation

When synthesizing multiple requirements into a scalable system design:

Comprehensive Document Analysis

Leverage the 200,000 token context to analyze entire research papers or technical specifications in a single request:

When to Choose Gemini 2.5 Flash

Real-time Chatbots and Customer Support

Immediate response is critical, and the cost savings are substantial:

Text Classification and Sentiment Analysis

Categorizing large volumes of emails, social media posts, and feedback:

Real-time Content Generation

Auto-generate news summaries, blog posts, and social media content:

Cost Impact Analysis

Let's examine the economic implications of model choice on a realistic workload:

Scenario: 1M monthly users, average 500-token input, 300-token output

  • Using Pro: ~$2,250/month
  • Using Flash: ~$750/month
  • Monthly Savings: $1,500 (67% reduction)

These savings can be reinvested in expanding user capacity, adding premium features, or improving infrastructure.

Smart Selection Strategy

  1. Start with Flash: Begin implementation with Flash; migrate to Pro only if accuracy requirements demand it
  2. Hybrid Approach: Use Flash for routine tasks, Pro for complex reasoning—same architecture, different models
  3. Context Awareness: Don't pay for 200K context when 100K suffices
  4. Validation Testing: Before production launch, run parallel tests with both models to verify quality alignment

Making the Right Choice

The decision between Gemini 2.5 Pro and Flash should be driven by your specific requirements:

  • Pro is better for: Research applications, complex code generation, document analysis, enterprise workflows
  • Flash is better for: Customer-facing features, real-time systems, high-volume processing, cost-sensitive applications

Most production systems benefit from a hybrid approach—using Flash for the bulk of operations and Pro for sophisticated tasks that demand advanced reasoning. This strategy optimizes both performance and cost efficiency.

en.title: Gemini Extensions Guide — Integrating with External Tools
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: Learn how to use Gemini Extensions to connect with external services and tools. From Google Search, Maps, YouTube, Flights, and Hotels integration to building custom extensions.
en.content:
What Are Gemini Extensions?

Gemini Extensions enable Gemini to access external services and data, providing more accurate and up-to-date information. In addition to built-in extensions, developers can build custom ones.

Available Standard Extensions

Google Search

Searches for the latest information and reflects it in responses. Automatically activated for questions requiring news, current events, or real-time data.

Examples:

  • "What's the weather in Tokyo today?"
  • "Summarize recent AI news"
  • "What are the latest earnings for company X?"

Google Maps

Searches for places, provides directions, and retrieves restaurant and hotel information.

Examples:

  • "How to get from Shibuya Station to Tokyo Tower"
  • "Best Italian restaurants nearby"
  • "Show hours and review ratings"

YouTube

Searches YouTube videos and suggests relevant content.

Examples:

  • "Find Python machine learning tutorial videos"
  • "Find the music video for this song"

Google Flights & Hotels

Search and compare flights and hotel accommodations.

Examples:

  • "Cheapest flights from Tokyo to Paris next month"
  • "Compare 4-star hotels in Barcelona"

Enabling Extensions

  1. Open Gemini settings
  2. Navigate to the "Extensions" section
  3. Toggle on the extensions you want to use

Each extension can be enabled or disabled individually.

Using Tools via API

Developers can use the tools parameter in the Gemini API to access similar functionality programmatically.

Custom Function Calling

Beyond standard extensions, you can define custom functions for Gemini to invoke.

Best Practices

  • Enable only needed extensions: Unnecessary extensions may trigger unintended external calls
  • Use grounding information: Verify source URLs from search results to validate reliability
  • Consider latency: Extension calls involve network access, increasing response time
  • Manage costs: API grounding searches may incur additional charges

Summary

Gemini Extensions dramatically expand what Gemini can do. Start with the built-in Google extensions, and as you get comfortable, build custom Function Calling integrations for your own tools and services.

en.title: Gemini for Business — Practical Tips to Boost Productivity
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: Learn how to maximize Gemini in business scenarios. From email drafting and document summarization to data analysis and meeting prep, discover practical techniques to dramatically improve daily workflows.
en.content:
Gemini as Your Business Partner

Google Gemini is more than a chatbot. With deep integration into Google Workspace, it functions as a powerful assistant across every aspect of your daily work. This article covers practical Gemini techniques that directly impact your productivity.

Streamlining Email Composition

Gemini in Gmail dramatically reduces the time spent drafting emails.

Example prompt:

Write a client email about sending a project proposal.
- Tone: Professional and concise
- Content: Website redesign project
- Attachment: Proposal PDF
- Next step: Schedule a meeting next week

The key is to specify the tone, content, and expected action clearly. Gemini understands context and generates appropriate business emails.

Document Summarization and Analysis

Gemini integrated into Google Docs excels at summarizing lengthy documents.

Use Cases

  • Meeting notes: Condense one hour of notes into three bullet points
  • Contract review: Extract key clauses and obligations
  • Report analysis: Pull conclusions and issues from lengthy reports
  • Competitive analysis: Compare insights across multiple documents

Effective Prompt Design

When requesting summaries, specifying the audience and purpose yields far more useful results.

Summarize this report for the executive team.
Organize by:
1. Top three achievements this quarter
2. Challenges and proposed solutions
3. Budget implications for next quarter

Data Analysis in Spreadsheets

Gemini in Google Sheets lowers the barrier to data analysis considerably.

  • Generate charts with natural language: "Show monthly revenue trends as a chart"
  • Auto-generate formulas: Create complex VLOOKUP or pivot tables from plain text
  • Data cleaning: Just describe what needs fixing — duplicates, formatting, etc.
  • Discover insights: "What trends stand out in this dataset?"

Automated Slide Creation

In Google Slides, Gemini auto-generates presentation drafts from text. It creates outlines and even suggests images. This is especially valuable for recurring reports and status updates where the format is already established.

Gemini for Google Workspace Pricing

To unlock the full potential of Gemini in business, you need Google Workspace Business plan or above.

| Plan | Gemini Features | |---|---| | Business Starter | Basic Gemini assist | | Business Standard | Advanced summarization & analysis | | Business Plus | Full access + NotebookLM | | Enterprise | Custom models + security controls |

Security and Privacy

The most important consideration for business use is data handling. Unlike the free version, Gemini in Google Workspace does not use your input data for model training. You can use it within an environment that complies with your organization's data governance policies.

Summary

The biggest advantage of adopting Gemini for business is its seamless integration with the Google Workspace tools you already use. With virtually zero learning curve, you can start seeing productivity gains immediately. Begin with everyday tasks like email drafting and document summarization, then expand from there.

en.title: Gemini Function Calling Complete Guide — External API Integration
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: Master function calling with Gemini API for seamless external API integration and agents
en.content:
Gemini Function Calling Complete Guide — External API Integration

Function calling elevates Gemini from a language model into an intelligent agent capable of interacting with external systems. By declaring functions (tools) that Gemini can call, you enable AI-driven automation, real-time data retrieval, and seamless integration with your existing APIs.

Understanding Function Calling

Function calling is a mechanism where:

  1. You define a set of functions/tools your application supports
  2. You provide these definitions to Gemini
  3. When appropriate, Gemini decides to call a function instead of generating text
  4. You execute the function and return the result to Gemini
  5. Gemini uses the result to formulate its next response

This creates a powerful loop where Gemini reasons about which tools to use and when to use them.

Why Function Calling Matters

Without function calling, a model can only provide information from its training data. Ask "What's the weather?" and you get a generic response.

With function calling, Gemini can:

  • Call a weather API to get current conditions
  • Query a database for real-time information
  • Process transactions through payment systems
  • Retrieve documents and analyze them
  • Perform complex calculations
  • Trigger automation workflows

This transforms Gemini into an intelligent intermediary between users and your systems.

Declaring Functions for Gemini

Function Declaration Structure

A function declaration tells Gemini what tools are available:

Key Components

name: The function name Gemini uses to reference the tool. Use snake_case, make it descriptive and unique.

description: Clear explanation of what the function does. Gemini reads this to decide whether to call it. Be concise but informative.

parameters: A JSON Schema object describing input parameters. This is critical:

  • Define each parameter's type and description
  • Mark required parameters
  • Include enums for restricted values
  • Provide examples in descriptions when helpful

Good vs. Poor Function Declarations

Poor declaration:

Good declaration:

Python Implementation

Setting Up Function Calling

Implementing Tool Functions

Create actual Python functions that do the work:

Full Function Calling Loop

TypeScript Implementation

Setting Up Functions in TypeScript

Function Implementation and Execution Loop

Advanced Patterns

Automatic Function Calling

For simpler use cases, enable automatic function calling where Gemini directly receives function results:

Combining Multiple Functions

Declare multiple related functions and let Gemini compose them:

Error Recovery

Handle function execution failures gracefully:

Best Practices

  1. Clear Declarations: Write precise function descriptions so Gemini understands when to use them

  2. Validate Inputs: Always validate function arguments before execution

  3. Meaningful Responses: Return structured, detailed results—Gemini uses these to reason

  4. Error Handling: Provide helpful error messages when functions fail

  5. Limited Tool Sets: Declare only the functions your application needs. Too many options confuses the model

  6. Test Thoroughly: Test function calling with various user inputs and edge cases

  7. Monitor Usage: Track which functions are called most frequently to optimize your toolkit

  8. Security: Validate that function calls are appropriate before execution. Never allow arbitrary code execution

Real-World Example: E-commerce Assistant

Conclusion

Function calling transforms Gemini from a knowledge model into an action agent. By properly declaring your tools and implementing robust execution logic, you unlock powerful AI-driven automation capabilities.

Start with a single critical function, perfect the implementation, then expand your toolkit. The flexibility of function calling means you can build everything from simple information retrieval systems to complex multi-step business processes.

The future of AI isn't just generation—it's intelligent reasoning with real-world action. Function calling is your bridge to that future.

en.title: Gemini Multimodal API Guide — Working with Images, Video, and Audio
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: Learn how to use Gemini API's multimodal capabilities for image recognition, video analysis, and audio processing. Includes Python/TypeScript code examples for working with non-text data.
en.content:
What Is Multimodal?

One of Gemini's greatest strengths is its ability to process multiple input types simultaneously — images, video, audio, PDFs, and more. This article covers how to implement multimodal processing with the Gemini API.

Supported Media Types

| Media | Formats | Max Size | |---|---|---| | Images | PNG, JPEG, WEBP, GIF | 20MB | | Video | MP4, MPEG, MOV, AVI | 2GB | | Audio | MP3, WAV, AIFF, FLAC | ~9.5 hours | | Documents | PDF | Up to 300 pages |

Image Examples

Sending with Base64 Encoding

Direct URL Reference

Images stored in Google Cloud Storage can be referenced directly by URI.

Video Analysis

Gemini can understand video content and provide summaries or answer questions about it.

Audio Processing

Transcription and summarization of audio files is also supported.

PDF Analysis

Upload PDF documents for content summarization and Q&A.

Combining Multiple Media

Gemini truly shines when processing multiple media types simultaneously.

Best Practices

  • Model selection: Use gemini-2.5-pro for high-accuracy analysis, gemini-2.5-flash for speed
  • File size: Use the File API for large files, inline_data for small ones
  • Prompt design: Specific instructions dramatically improve accuracy even with multimodal input
  • Cost management: Video and audio consume more tokens — trim to essential segments

Summary

Gemini's multimodal API enables use cases that were impossible with text alone. From image recognition and video summarization to audio transcription, tasks that once required specialized tools can now be handled through a unified API.

en.title: Google AI Studio Guide — Try the Gemini API for Free
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: Complete guide to Google AI Studio for prototyping Gemini API without coding setup
en.content:
Google AI Studio Guide — Try the Gemini API for Free

Google AI Studio is a browser-based platform that democratizes access to the Gemini API. Whether you're a non-technical user curious about AI capabilities or a developer prototyping before writing code, AI Studio provides an intuitive interface to experiment with Gemini models without requiring any development setup.

What is Google AI Studio?

AI Studio is Google's free, web-based tool for interacting with the Gemini API. It eliminates setup friction—no API keys to manage initially, no SDKs to install, no authentication headers to configure. You can start experimenting with Gemini immediately through an elegant, user-friendly interface.

Think of AI Studio as a playground for AI development. It's where ideas become prototypes, where you can validate approaches before investing engineering resources, and where you can learn how Gemini works without technical barriers.

Accessing Google AI Studio

Getting started is straightforward:

  1. Visit aistudio.google.com in your web browser
  2. Sign in with your Google account
  3. Start creating and testing immediately

No signup fees, no lengthy onboarding, no credit card required. The free tier provides generous usage limits suitable for learning, prototyping, and light production use.

Core Features of AI Studio

Chat Interface

The primary way to interact with Gemini in AI Studio. Type prompts and receive responses in a conversational format. This is perfect for:

  • Exploring Gemini's capabilities
  • Testing different prompt approaches
  • Having multi-turn conversations
  • Understanding how the model behaves

Model Selection

AI Studio lets you choose between Gemini models:

  • Gemini 2.5 Pro: For complex reasoning and analysis
  • Gemini 2.5 Flash: For faster responses and high-volume use
  • Gemini 1.5 Pro: Previous generation with strong performance
  • Gemini 1.5 Flash: Efficient alternative for production

Switch between models to compare performance and choose the best fit for your use case.

System Instructions

One of AI Studio's most powerful features is the ability to define system instructions. These are guidelines that shape how Gemini behaves for your specific use case.

System instructions allow you to:

  • Define the model's role or persona
  • Set tone and style preferences
  • Establish boundaries and constraints
  • Specify output format requirements
  • Inject domain knowledge or context

Example System Instruction:

You are an expert Python developer with 10 years of experience.
Help users write clean, efficient, well-documented Python code.
Always explain your reasoning and suggest improvements.
Always use type hints and follow PEP 8 standards.

Structured Prompts

Rather than free-form text, you can create structured prompts with organized fields. This is especially useful for:

  • Consistent input templates
  • Ensuring all required information is provided
  • Building reusable prompt patterns
  • Facilitating collaboration

You can define custom fields like:

  • Input (the main content to process)
  • Context (background information)
  • Format (desired output structure)
  • Constraints (specific requirements)

Working with Prompts in AI Studio

Creating a Basic Prompt

  1. Click "Create new" and select "Chat" or "Prompt"
  2. Enter your prompt text in the compose area
  3. Click "Send" to execute
  4. Review the response and iterate

Refining Your Prompts

AI Studio encourages an iterative approach:

  • Start simple: Begin with a basic prompt to understand the model's baseline
  • Add context: Include relevant background information
  • Specify output: Be explicit about what format or structure you want
  • Test variations: Try different phrasings to optimize results

Using System Instructions Effectively

System instructions are context that applies to your entire conversation. Good system instructions:

  • Are specific and clear about role and expectations
  • Provide concrete guidelines without being overly restrictive
  • Include examples when helpful
  • Are reasonable in length (concise but complete)
You are an expert technical writer specializing in software documentation.
Write clear, concise explanations suitable for developers.
Use code examples when relevant.
Organize information in a logical, scannable format.
Use markdown syntax for formatting.

Examples and Few-Shot Learning

Provide examples of desired output to guide the model:

Input Example:

Sentiment Analysis Task

Analyze the sentiment of the following text and provide a score from 1-5
(1=very negative, 5=very positive)

Examples:
Text: "I love this product! Works perfectly!" → Sentiment: 5
Text: "Decent, but could be better." → Sentiment: 3

Now analyze:
Text: [user input]

Exporting Your Work

When you've perfected a prompt in AI Studio, you can export it for use elsewhere:

Export to Code

Generate starter code for:

  • Python (using google-genai)
  • TypeScript (using @google/genai)
  • cURL commands
  • REST API calls

This is invaluable for transitioning from prototyping to production development.

Save and Share

  • Save prompts to your AI Studio library
  • Share public prompts with team members
  • Organize by project or category
  • Version control your prompts

API Key Management

When you're ready to use Gemini outside of AI Studio, you'll need an API key:

  1. Navigate to the API key section in AI Studio
  2. Create a new API key
  3. Copy the key securely
  4. Store it in your application's environment variables (never hardcode keys)

Security best practices:

  • Never share your API key in version control
  • Use environment variables (.env files) for local development
  • Rotate keys periodically
  • Delete unused keys
  • Monitor your API usage for unexpected activity

Free Tier Limits

AI Studio's free tier includes:

  • 60 requests per minute
  • 1.5 million tokens per month (for text)
  • 1000 requests per day
  • Image uploads and processing
  • Full access to all models

These limits are sufficient for prototyping and learning. For production applications with higher volume, consider upgrading to a paid plan.

Best Practices for AI Studio

1. Be Specific

The more specific your prompt, the better the results. Instead of "summarize this article," try "summarize this article in 3 bullet points, focusing on the key findings and business implications."

2. Provide Context

Include relevant background information that helps Gemini understand your specific needs and domain.

3. Test Multiple Approaches

Don't settle on the first working prompt. Try variations to find what works best for your use case.

4. Use System Instructions Strategically

System instructions are best for consistent guidance that applies across multiple conversations. Save per-prompt specifics for the main prompt.

5. Document Your Workflow

Take notes on what works and why. This accelerates the transition from prototyping to production.

Transitioning from AI Studio to API

Once you've validated your approach in AI Studio:

  1. Export the code from AI Studio
  2. Set up your development environment
  3. Install the appropriate SDK (Python or TypeScript)
  4. Implement error handling and production safeguards
  5. Test thoroughly before deploying

The prompts and system instructions you perfect in AI Studio translate directly to your API implementation.

Conclusion

Google AI Studio removes barriers to AI development. It's the ideal starting point whether you're exploring AI capabilities for the first time or validating complex AI solutions before building them at scale.

Spend time experimenting. Test different models. Refine your prompts. Discover what's possible. Then, when you're ready, export your work and scale it using the Gemini API.

Visit aistudio.google.com today and start your AI journey. The best part? It's completely free to get started.

en.title: Grounding with Google Search — Improve Gemini's Accuracy with Search
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: Learn how to use Gemini API's Grounding with Google Search to generate accurate, up-to-date responses. Covers Dynamic Retrieval, source citations, and cost management.
en.content:
What Is Grounding?

One of the biggest weaknesses of LLMs is "hallucination" — generating responses that sound plausible but are factually incorrect or outdated. Grounding with Google Search addresses this by anchoring Gemini's responses in actual Google search results.

Basic Usage

With this simple configuration, Gemini automatically performs Google searches and generates responses based on the results.

Using Grounding Metadata

Source information is returned as grounding_metadata.

Dynamic Retrieval

Not every request needs a search. Dynamic Retrieval lets Gemini automatically decide when searching is necessary.

Adjust dynamic_threshold to control search sensitivity:

  • 0.0: Always search
  • 0.5: Balanced threshold (recommended default)
  • 1.0: Rarely search

Comparison with Custom RAG

| Aspect | Grounding with Google Search | Custom RAG | |---|---|---| | Data source | Google search index | Your own database | | Setup | Zero (API parameter only) | Requires vector DB | | Freshness | Real-time | Depends on update frequency | | Cost | Per API usage | Infrastructure + API costs | | Customization | Low | High | | Internal data | Not available | Supported |

Implementation Patterns

News Summary Bot

Fact Checker

Cost Management

Grounding with Google Search is powerful but incurs additional cost per request.

Optimization tips:

  • Use Dynamic Retrieval to avoid unnecessary searches
  • Disable grounding for creative tasks that don't need fact-checking
  • For batch processing, generate answers without search first, then apply grounding only to low-confidence sections

Limitations

  • Search results depend on Google's search index — unindexed information cannot be retrieved
  • Internal or private data is inaccessible (use custom RAG for those cases)
  • Search result language is influenced by the prompt language

Summary

Grounding with Google Search dramatically improves the accuracy of Gemini's responses. It's essential for use cases where up-to-date information and fact-checking matter. Combined with Dynamic Retrieval, you can optimize the balance between cost and accuracy.

en.title: What is Google Gemini? Features and Getting Started Guide
en.author: Gemini Lab
en.description: Complete guide to Google Gemini: models, capabilities, features, and how to get started with AI
en.content:
What is Google Gemini? Features and Getting Started Guide

Google Gemini is a cutting-edge artificial intelligence system developed by Google DeepMind that represents the next generation of AI capabilities. Launched in late 2023, Gemini has rapidly become one of the most powerful and versatile AI models available, offering breakthrough performance across text, image, audio, and video understanding.

What Makes Gemini Different?

Gemini stands out in the AI landscape for several key reasons. Unlike previous models that excelled in specific domains, Gemini was built from the ground up as a truly multimodal system. This means it can seamlessly process and understand multiple types of content simultaneously—text, images, audio, and video all together.

The architecture of Gemini incorporates advanced training techniques that give it superior reasoning capabilities. Whether you're asking it to analyze complex documents, generate creative content, write code, or solve mathematical problems, Gemini approaches tasks with remarkable accuracy and nuance.

Gemini Models Overview

Google offers several versions of Gemini, each optimized for different use cases:

Gemini 2.5 Pro

The flagship model designed for complex, multi-step tasks requiring advanced reasoning. Gemini 2.5 Pro excels at:

  • Deep analysis of documents and code
  • Complex problem-solving and research
  • Long-form content generation with coherence and accuracy
  • Multimodal understanding across texts, images, and more

Gemini 2.5 Flash

A lightweight, faster model perfect for high-volume applications where speed matters. Ideal for:

  • Real-time chat and conversational AI
  • Quick content generation and summarization
  • High-throughput API applications
  • Mobile and edge deployments

Core Capabilities

Text Generation

Gemini can generate everything from emails and marketing copy to technical documentation and creative fiction. It understands context, maintains tone consistency, and produces human-quality text across diverse domains.

Image Understanding and Generation

Process images to extract information, answer questions about visual content, and generate new images based on text descriptions. This capability opens possibilities for document analysis, accessibility tools, and creative applications.

Code Generation and Analysis

Write, debug, and explain code across multiple programming languages. Gemini understands programming paradigms, can refactor code for efficiency, and helps developers learn best practices.

Multimodal Processing

The true power of Gemini lies in its ability to process multiple input types together. Feed it a document with images and charts, ask a question, and it understands the complete context.

Getting Started with Gemini

Web Interface: Gemini.google.com

The easiest way to experience Gemini is through the web interface at gemini.google.com. No setup required—simply visit the site and start chatting.

Features of the web interface:

  • Free access to Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash
  • Conversation history and saved chats
  • Image upload and analysis
  • Clean, intuitive interface
  • Works on desktop and mobile devices

Creating a Google Account

You'll need a Google account to access Gemini. If you don't have one, creating an account is free and takes just a few minutes.

Gemini Plans: Free vs Paid

Free Plan

  • Access to Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash via web interface
  • Limited daily usage
  • Perfect for exploring and learning
  • Image upload and processing included

Gemini Advanced (Paid)

  • Higher usage limits
  • Priority access to new features
  • Enhanced daily quotas
  • Integrated with Google Workspace
  • Available for personal use and organizations

API Access

Developers can access Gemini through the Google AI API with generous free tier limits and flexible pricing for production applications.

Key Advantages of Using Gemini

Accuracy and Reliability: Gemini's advanced training enables it to provide factually grounded responses with appropriate confidence levels. When uncertain, it acknowledges limitations rather than fabricating information.

Efficiency: Gemini 2.5 Flash delivers remarkable performance without requiring premium resources, making it cost-effective for high-volume applications.

Safety by Design: Google has implemented robust safety measures including:

  • Content filtering and moderation systems
  • Bias detection and mitigation
  • Privacy protection and data handling compliance
  • Transparent safety policies and documentation

Versatility: A single model that handles text, images, code, and reasoning means developers can build more comprehensive applications without juggling multiple specialized models.

Use Cases for Gemini

Gemini's capabilities make it ideal for:

  • Education: Personalized tutoring, homework assistance, and learning support
  • Business: Customer service, content creation, data analysis, and automation
  • Development: Code generation, debugging, documentation, and technical support
  • Creative Work: Writing, brainstorming, design ideation, and content generation
  • Research: Document analysis, summarization, and knowledge synthesis

Getting Help and Learning More

Google provides extensive documentation, tutorials, and community resources:

  • Official Google AI documentation with guides and best practices
  • Community forums for questions and knowledge sharing
  • Blog posts announcing new features and capabilities
  • Interactive tutorials and sample projects

Conclusion

Google Gemini represents a significant leap forward in AI capabilities. Whether you're a student exploring AI for the first time, a developer building AI-powered applications, or a professional looking to enhance your productivity, Gemini offers powerful tools and impressive versatility.

Start exploring at gemini.google.com today. The free tier provides ample opportunity to discover what Gemini can do for your specific needs. As you grow more comfortable with the platform, you can explore advanced features, API integration, and more sophisticated use cases.

The future of AI is here, and Gemini is leading the way. Embrace the possibilities and see how this transformative technology can enhance your work and creativity.
